# UC-18.2.14 · NSX Intelligence Top Flows and Anomalous East-West Volume

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Performance, Security, Anomaly &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch sideways chatter inside private-cloud racks—not only outward Internet exits—and compare each week's habits so unusual east-west bursts tied to logged-in people ring alarms early. Teams tighten partitions sooner instead of discovering stray workstation hops months later.*

---

## Description

VMware NSX Intelligence is a standalone analytics plane (Intelligence Node beside NSX-T 3.x managers; NAPP-hosted services in NSX 4.x). It ingests IPFIX and punted-flow metadata from ESXi vsip into Apache Druid OLAP slices with src_VM, dst_VM, addresses, ports, protocol, bytes, packets, optional IDFW process_name and user_name fields, anomaly_score, East-West versus North-South classification labels, dynamic security-group identifiers, TTL-bound flow-table retention, visualization-graph payloads served at `/policy/api/v1/intelligence/flows/visualization-graph`, recommendation queues at `/policy/api/v1/intelligence/recommendations`, correlations at `/policy/api/v1/intelligence/correlations`, group flow-stats at `/policy/api/v1/intelligence/groups/{group_id}/flow-stats`, and optional Druid SQL via `/api/v1/napp/druid/v2/sql`. Splunk Add-on for VMware NSX-T (Splunkbase 4856) sourcetype vmware:nsxt:intelligence:flow lands correlated flows across ESXi hosts so teams reconcile Splunk rankings with Intelligence UI Visualization before segmentation drift hides lateral noise.

## Value

NSX Intelligence ranks overlay flows and anomaly scores per security-group pair so operators catch dominant east-west chatter, statistical outliers above 0.85, and Identity Firewall identity bursts before micro-segmentation recommendations stall in publish queues—closing the gap between Intelligence Visualization graphs and actionable Splunk alerts.

## Implementation

Deploy Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt ([Splunkbase 4856](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) with modular inputs or HEC endpoints landing vmware:nsxt:intelligence:flow plus vmware:nsxt:napp:status; map HTTPS collectors for Intelligence Policy routes into auxiliary indexes; schedule hourly searches scoped per tenant dashboard token; correlate anomalies with ServiceNow CHG lookups before paging. Quick win: ship vmware:nsxt:intelligence:flow alongside visualization-graph JSON snapshots before Druid SQL parity macros.

## Evidence

Saved search uc_18_2_14_nsx_intel_top_flows.json plus dashboard NSX Intelligence — East-West Evidence exporting PNG/CSV bundles tagged by pair_key and tenant token.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Install **Splunk_TA_vmware-nsxt** ([Splunkbase 4856](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4856)) on **Search Heads**, **Heavy Forwarders** hosting **REST** collectors, and **HEC** endpoints receiving **NSX Intelligence** exports for **VMware NSX 4.x** estates where Intelligence is activated on **NSX Application Platform** (**NAPP**).
- Provision **least-privilege** API identities able to call **Policy** routes under `/policy/api/v1/intelligence/` plus optional Manager telemetry when **gRPC NSX Telemetry** exporters supplement Splunk-side correlation.
- Guarantee collectors reach **HTTPS** endpoints on **NSX Manager** or Policy VIPs over **TCP 443** without rewriting **JSON bodies**—**FIELDALIAS** stanzas assume stable keys (`src_vm`, `dst_vm`, **group identifiers**).
- Budget indexes so hourly stats across dense east-west meshes remain inside indexer CPU envelopes—**vmware:nsxt:intelligence:flow** volume scales with workload cardinality and **vsip** sampling cadence tied to Intelligence **collection policies**.

**Quick win:** Mirror GET `/policy/api/v1/intelligence/flows/visualization-graph` snapshots into Splunk alongside vmware:nsxt:intelligence:flow before layering `/api/v1/napp/druid/v2/sql` parity macros—operators validate dominant pairs against **Intelligence Visualization** screenshots within one sprint.

### Step 1 — Data sources
(1) REST visualization hub — Poll `/policy/api/v1/intelligence/flows/visualization-graph` for **graph payloads** summarizing **correlated flows** suitable for **Splunk lookups** keyed by **flow_id** fragments plus vertex membership hints referenced inside Intelligence **Visualization** tabs.
(2) Group-level throughput — Capture `/policy/api/v1/intelligence/groups/{group_id}/flow-stats` responses so dynamic membership swings reconcile inside Splunk when **dynamic groups** or **security tags** move VMs faster than **CMDB** tickets refresh.
(3) Recommendation backlog — Mirror `/policy/api/v1/intelligence/recommendations` JSON into Splunk (HTTPS modular inputs or scripted forwarders) to trend publish-queue depth beside **recommended_rule_id** fields arriving inside vmware:nsxt:intelligence:flow extracts.
(4) Correlation services — Subscribe `/policy/api/v1/intelligence/correlations` where deployments expose composite scenarios—align `_time` stamps with vmware:nsxt:intelligence:flow bursts to avoid misblaming single-host anomalies.
(5) **Druid** OLAP — Where licensed, issue controlled POST `/api/v1/napp/druid/v2/sql` queries against **Apache Druid** backing NAPP services; isolate read-only identities and clamp result sizes to protect **NAPP pods**.
(6) **IPFIX** collector offload — When Intelligence ingests brokered IPFIX from stand-alone collectors, land those streams with the same flow_id and address keys the **Splunk TA** expects so **ESXi** host diversity collapses cleanly in stats.
(7) TA sourcetype spine — Rely on vmware:nsxt:intelligence:flow for cross-host correlation documented under **Splunkbase** 4856, augmenting with **vmware:nsxt:syslog** when administrators toggle Intelligence collection knobs.
(8) NAPP posture — Index **vmware:nsxt:napp:status** for NAPP pod readiness, Druid **broker cues**, and upgrade chatter tied to **Intelligence downtime** windows so Splunk distinguishes ingestion pauses from stealthy silence.
(9) Telemetry augmentation — Optional gRPC **NSX Telemetry** streams complement—not replace—REST snapshots when verifying backlog discrepancies during **Sev-3 bridges**.

### Step 2 — Field extractions
Normalize FIELDALIAS chains so flow_id, **src_vm_id**, **dst_vm_id**, src_ip, dst_ip, src_port, dst_port, **l4_proto**, **l7_app_id**, **bytes_per_second**, **packets_per_second**, **conn_count**, **group_id_src**, **group_id_dst**, **recommended_rule_id**, **anomaly_score**, **classification** (**East-West** versus **North-South**), **idfw_user**, and **process_name** reconcile camelCase REST payloads with syslog fragments—duplicate **Inventory** entities break downstream CMDB joins.

### Step 3 — Initial validation
Cross-check **Splunk aggregates** against NSX Intelligence UI Visualization tabs enumerating dominant flows—screenshots anchored by flow_id settle debates faster than reconstructed guesses.
Validate suspicious classification ratios (East-West dominance) against **vRealize Network Insight** **vrni:flow:netflow** streams before opening sev bridges.
On the ESXi host servicing a suspect VM pair, optional nsxdp-cli ipfix flows show output confirms vsip export behavior when REST and Splunk disagree about short spikes.
Compare `/policy/api/v1/intelligence/recommendations` backlog depth captured in Splunk with Policy UI queues to ensure scripted collectors remain authenticated—HTTP 401 spikes often masquerade as traffic mysteries.

### Step 4 — SPL building blocks
- Top talker ranking — stats sum(bytes_per_second) by group_id_src and group_id_dst with bin _time span=1h to highlight sustained pairings that outrank historical peers.
- Baseline deviation — eventstats perc95 per **pair_key**, then compare live bytes_per_second to rolling baselines while requiring anomaly_score>0.85 before paging because Intelligence attaches model-driven corroboration to raw throughput spikes.
- East-West anomaly slices — Filter classification tokens referencing east-west semantics; aggregate sum(bytes_per_second) every fifteen minutes per pair_key built from group_id_src::group_id_dst.
- Recommendation queue trending — Separate scheduled search counting outstanding recommended_rule_id candidates correlated with vmware:nsxt:intelligence:flow spikes showing anomaly_score crests—queues balloon after Policy publishes churn or draft edits recycle.
- IDFW correlation — Chart bytes_per_second attributed to idfw_user values versus anonymous flows—large idfw_weighted_bps fractions spotlight interactive lateral movement suspects deserving **Identity Firewall** tuning reviews.

### Step 5 — Comprehensive enrichment patterns (deepest section)
Bind Splunk Add-on for VMware ([Splunkbase 1810](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1810)) guest inventories so src_vm_id rows inherit business-criticality tiers exported from **vCenter** custom attributes—critical databases suppress benign-but-noisy anomaly chatter automatically via **lookup thresholds**.
Attach CMDB owning squad tables keyed off VM tags pulled alongside vCenter facts—east-west anomalies inherit accountable owners instead of anonymous infrastructure bridges.
Join **ServiceNow** change payloads tagged CHG categories referencing application onboarding waves—Splunk overlays bytes_per_second spikes against planned migrations before **SOC** escalations consume scarce bridges.
Map normalized flows into **CIM Network_Traffic** (src, dest, bytes, transport) plus **Performance** proxies where bytes_per_second fields mimic throughput KPI children feeding executive dashboards—accelerate models only after FIELDALIAS parity passes QA.
Provide **Splunk Enterprise Security** correlation searches referencing MITRE ATT&CK technique **T1071** (Application Layer Protocol) when process_name plus idfw_user anomalies coincide with recommended_rule backlog churn indicative of scripted **lateral relays**—not merely noisy backups.
Augment suspicious l7_app_id spikes with MITRE ATT&CK technique **T1568** (Dynamic Resolution) narratives only after DNS telemetry complements Intelligence summaries—avoid standalone accusations from flow metadata alone.
Track recommended_rule realization by comparing recommended_rule_id arrivals with **Distributed Firewall** publish confirmations—**Splunk timelines** prove whether drafts matured into enforced segmentation or stalled inside Policy drafts waiting on **CAB approvals**.
Monitor NAPP capacity headroom using vmware:nsxt:napp:status pod recycling counters plus **appendcols** summaries adjacent to vmware:nsxt:intelligence:flow panels—when Druid ingestion saturates, anomaly scoring degrades before switches overflow.
Tune **Splunk ES risk-based alerting** templates so pair_key entities scoring **peak_anom**>0.85 populate **risk.notable** rows enriched with group_id_src ownership lookups—SOC analysts pivot without rebuilding dashboards manually.
Document quarterly governance reviews covering Intelligence retention TTL shrink/expansion decisions because **TTL-bound** **flow tables** influence whether Splunk baselines represent comparable horizons—misaligned TTL versus Splunk lookbacks exaggerates false East-West anomalies during compressed retention pilots.

### Step 6 — Operational runbook
Treat sustained **ew_volume_anomaly**=1 indicators plus corroborating **baseline_spike** flags as **Sev-3** candidates routed to platform networking crews—suppress using approved ServiceNow bridges referencing **onboarding calendars** before paging overnight responders.
Wire **micro-segmentation** publish loops: once recommended_rule candidates graduate **Policy QA**, attach Splunk notable suppression tokens referencing recommended_rule_id until realization probes confirm enforced rules across clusters—failure loops reopen dashboards automatically when classification anomalies persist post-publish.
Operational KPI targets: maintain **MTBF** exceeding ninety days between repeat Intelligence ingestion regressions tied solely to vmware:nsxt:napp:status fault loops; keep **MTTR** for Intelligence service degradation (Druid ingestion stalls, **napp_events** collapses to zero while flows continue) under thirty minutes via documented **escalation paths** to **VMware support bundles** with Intelligence + NAPP diagnostics.
Tenant-specific **dashboard tokens** isolate east-west anomalies per business unit—**tokenized panels** include top-talker pairs, East-West versus North-South splits, **anomaly-score heatmaps**, recommendation-queue depth, and IDFW **identity leaderboards** referencing Splunk lookups for service owners.

Governance & privacy
Collector credentials often possess **Enterprise Admin** scopes in lab settings—production should instead use **Auditor**-plus Intelligence read roles, store secrets inside **Splunk credential services**, and rotate API keys whenever NAPP **TLS certificates** renew so REST scrapes survive without interactive reauth.

Documentation anchors
Archive hourly CSV extracts of priority pair_key rows plus corresponding Intelligence Visualization PNGs for audit evidence—proves Splunk mirrored API truth rather than synthetic replays.


## SPL

```spl
(index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx OR index=network_ops)
sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:intelligence:flow" earliest=-24h@m
| eval flow_id=trim(coalesce(flow_id,intel_flow_uid,flow_guid,intelligence_flow_key,""))
| eval src_vm_id=trim(coalesce(src_vm_id,src_vm_uuid,src_vm_entity,src_vm_res_id,""))
| eval dst_vm_id=trim(coalesce(dst_vm_id,dst_vm_uuid,dst_vm_entity,dst_vm_res_id,""))
| eval group_id_src=trim(coalesce(group_id_src,src_sec_group,src_dyn_group,src_group_uuid,""))
| eval group_id_dst=trim(coalesce(group_id_dst,dst_sec_group,dst_dyn_group,dst_group_uuid,""))
| eval anomaly_score=tonumber(coalesce(anomaly_score,intel_anomaly_scr,anomaly_ml,flow_risk_scr,"0"))
| eval bytes_per_second=tonumber(coalesce(bytes_per_second,flow_bps,bps_metric,byte_rate_per_sec,"0"))
| eval classification=upper(trim(coalesce(classification,traf_bucket,ew_ns_label,intel_direction,"")))
| eval idfw_user=trim(coalesce(idfw_user,user_name,idfw_login,idfw_principal,""))
| eval proc_hint=trim(coalesce(process_name,app_proc,intel_proc_name,""))
| eval pair_key=if(len(group_id_src)>0 AND len(group_id_dst)>0,group_id_src."::".group_id_dst,"unsegmented_pair")
| eval ew_hit=if(match(classification,"(?i)EAST") OR match(classification,"(?i)EW"),1,0)
| eval ns_hit=if(match(classification,"(?i)NORTH") OR match(classification,"(?i)NS"),1,0)
| eval anom_flag=if(anomaly_score>0.85,1,0)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats sum(bytes_per_second) as sum_bps max(anomaly_score) as peak_anom dc(flow_id) as flow_variety
    sum(eval(if(ew_hit=1,bytes_per_second,0))) as ew_bps_sum sum(eval(if(ns_hit=1,bytes_per_second,0))) as ns_bps_sum
    sum(anom_flag) as anom_segments sum(eval(if(len(idfw_user)>0,bytes_per_second,0))) as idfw_weighted_bps
    values(idfw_user) as idfw_users values(proc_hint) as proc_hints values(flow_id) as flow_examples count as evt_qty
  by pair_key group_id_src group_id_dst src_vm_id dst_vm_id _time
| fillnull value=0 sum_bps ew_bps_sum ns_bps_sum idfw_weighted_bps peak_anom
| eventstats perc95(sum_bps) as p95_pair by pair_key
| eval baseline_spike=if(sum_bps>(1.5*p95_pair) AND peak_anom>0.85 AND isnotnull(p95_pair),1,0)
| eval ew_volume_anomaly=if(ew_bps_sum>(0.6*p95_pair) AND peak_anom>0.5 AND ew_bps_sum>1000 AND isnotnull(p95_pair),1,0)
| eval idfw_burst=if(idfw_weighted_bps>(0.35*sum_bps) AND mvcount(idfw_users)>0,1,0)
| sort 0 group_id_src _time - sum_bps
| streamstats count as talk_rank_within_src_grp by group_id_src _time
| sort 0 - sum_bps
| head 200
| appendcols max=0 [
    search (index=vmware OR index=vmware_nsx) sourcetype="vmware:nsxt:napp:status" earliest=-24h@m
    | stats count as napp_events dc(host) as napp_hosts latest(_time) as napp_last_seen
      max(eval(if(match(lower(_raw),"(?i)druid"),1,0))) as druid_hint
  ]
| table _time pair_key group_id_src group_id_dst src_vm_id dst_vm_id sum_bps peak_anom baseline_spike ew_volume_anomaly ew_bps_sum ns_bps_sum idfw_weighted_bps idfw_burst talk_rank_within_src_grp flow_variety anom_segments idfw_users proc_hints napp_events napp_hosts napp_last_seen druid_hint
```

## Visualization

(1) Column/bar chart — top-talking pair_key rows ranked by sum(bytes_per_second) per rolling hour with drilldown to flow_id samples. (2) Pie or treemap — East-West versus North-South share of bytes_per_second split by tenant token. (3) Heatmap — anomaly_score versus fifteen-minute buckets colored by baseline_spike/ew_volume_anomaly pairs. (4) Single-value plus sparkline — outstanding recommended_rule_id backlog versus Policy publish timestamps with threshold ribbon. (5) Table — IDFW identity leaderboard correlating idfw_user with bytes_per_second plus proc_hint values for SOC pivots.

## Known False Positives

**Wave-scheduled workload onboarding** — Parallel cutovers toward freshly segmented tiers deliberately inflate Intelligence rankings because health checks and staged database copies open short-lived sockets between cohorts sharing the same dynamic group tag; classify as planned variance once change tickets cite the rollout window and Intelligence classification alternates North-South then East-West inside that sanctioned interval rather than drifting slowly across unrelated business days. **Quarter-end ledger and reporting surges** — Finance and procurement jobs open large SQL and file-copy conversations between VMs that rarely talk; anomaly scores climb even though malware is absent—anchor suppressions on fiscal calendar fixtures and the business owner of the batch stream. **Backup-window replication dominance** — Dedicated backup proxies stream east-west toward media servers and object stores during nightly windows; byte-weighted charts crown those pairs while anomaly scores stay low—correlate with backup catalog schedules before paging micro-segmentation teams. **Recommendation queue recycling** — Published NSX Intelligence recommendations that re-enter the `/policy/api/v1/intelligence/recommendations` backlog after Distributed Firewall edits or group renames show queue-depth churn unrelated to stealthy traffic—validate against recent publish actions in Policy before treating depth as compromise. **NAPP-Druid segment maintenance** — During NSX Application Platform upgrades, Intelligence services pause or rebuild Druid segments; flow exports may stutter while NAPP pods recycle, temporarily suppressing anomaly_score math or fragmenting totals—inspect vmware:nsxt:napp:status plus appliance health surfaces before declaring data exfiltration.
